text.tspan: Six read-the-room principles, traced to the seven things the product must actually do. Orange marks the primary driver; the
Always show where you are in the scene and the view.
Make mode, selection, and context continuously visible.
Show only what the current task needs; defer the rest.
Explain tools in place, at the moment they are used.
A wrong move should never feel unrecoverable.
Engineer a finished object inside the first session.

g.text: Six principles → seven requirements → one coherent first-hour system. Nothing in the build exists without a reason traceable to observed behaviour.
